He'd grow up to become a legendary linebacker, first shining at the University of Oklahoma. There, he helped his team clinch the National Championship in 1985. Later, Bosworth took his talents to the NFL, playing for the Seattle Seahawks.
activity_question: What was Brian Bosworth's nickname?
bonus_fact: Bosworth's flashy style and bold personality made him stand out both on and off the field.

title: A Voice for Change Born in Boston
story: In a Boston neighborhood, a future champion for peace took his first breath. Michael Patrick MacDonald was born in 1966, destined to become a powerful voice against crime and violence. Growing up in Southie, he saw firsthand the struggles of his community. MacDonald turned his experiences into a gripping book, shining a light on important issues. His birth on March 9 marked the start of a journey to make positive changes in the world.
activity_question: What did Michael Patrick MacDonald write about?
follow_up_question: How do you think writing about his experiences might help others?

title: From Mississippi to NBA Stardom
story: Dribbling his way into basketball history, Mahmoud Abdul-Rauf was born in Gulfport, Mississippi on March 9, 1969. This talented guard would go on to play for NBA teams like the Sacramento Kings and Denver Nuggets, thrilling fans with his quick moves and sharp shooting skills.
activity_question: Which NBA teams did Mahmoud Abdul-Rauf play for?
activity_choices: Los Angeles Lakers and Chicago Bulls
New York Knicks and Golden State Warriors

title: Tech Whiz Kid Grows Up
story: Gadgets and gizmos fascinated young David Pogue from the start. Born in Shaker Heights, Ohio on March 9, 1963, he would grow up to become a famous technology expert. Pogue now explains tricky tech topics in a fun way, writing newspaper columns and hosting TV shows about science and cool inventions.
activity_question: What does David Pogue do now?
follow_up_question: What kind of shows does David Pogue host on TV?

title: From Action Movies to Disney Magic
story: Mark Mancina, the musical genius behind thrilling action films and enchanting Disney tunes, was born in Santa Monica, California in 1957. On March 9 that year, a future composer entered the world. Mancina would go on to create unforgettable scores for movies like Speed, Bad Boys, and the beloved animated adventure Moana.
activity_question: What Disney animated movie did Mark Mancina compose for?
follow_up_question: Can you name another action movie Mark Mancina composed for?

title: The Boy Emperor Who Changed China
story: A young prince named Liu Che became Emperor Wu of Han on March 9, 141 BC. At just 16 years old, he took charge of China's powerful Han Dynasty. Emperor Wu ruled for a very long time, shaping China's future. He expanded the empire, opened trade routes, and encouraged new ideas in art and learning.
activity_question: How old was Liu Che when he became Emperor Wu of Han?
follow_up_question: Can you imagine being in charge of a whole country at such a young age?

title: Cabral's Accidental Discovery of Brazil
story: Sails billowed as Pedro Álvares Cabral led a fleet of 13 ships out of Lisbon's harbor on March 9, 1500. His mission? To reach India and establish trade routes. But fate had other plans. Veering off course, Cabral's expedition stumbled upon a new land - Brazil! This unexpected detour would change history, as Portugal claimed the vast territory for its empire.
activity_question: What was Cabral's original mission when he left Lisbon?
follow_up_question: How many ships were in Cabral's fleet when it left Lisbon?

title: A Powerful Opera Takes the Stage
story: The audience held their breath as the curtain rose. Giuseppe Verdi's new opera, Nabucco, was about to begin. On March 9, 1842, the powerful music filled Milan's theater. Nabucco told the story of ancient Babylon and became a huge success. It launched Verdi's career as one of the greatest opera composers in history.
activity_question: What was the name of Giuseppe Verdi's opera that premiered?
follow_up_question: What famous Italian city did Nabucco premiere in?

title: Mahler's Musical Marvel Debuts
story: Thunderous applause filled the concert hall in Berlin, Germany. Music lovers were hearing something extraordinary for the first time. On March 9, 1897, parts of Gustav Mahler's massive 3rd Symphony premiered. This wasn't just any piece of music. Mahler's symphony was a musical journey through nature, lasting nearly two hours when played in full!
activity_question: Where did parts of Mahler's 3rd Symphony premiere?
bonus_fact: Mahler's 3rd Symphony is one of the longest symphonies ever written in the standard repertoire.

title: Baseball Star Turns Down Big Bucks
story: Crack! Ted Williams' bat sent baseballs soaring, making him a superstar. In 1946, the Mexican Baseball League tried to lure him away with a huge offer. On March 9, they dangled $500,000 - an enormous sum back then. But Williams said no, choosing to stay loyal to his American team.
activity_question: What did Ted Williams refuse?
activity_choices: $500,000 to play in Mexican Baseball League
activity_answer: $500,000 to play in Mexican Baseball League
follow_up_question: Why do you think loyalty might be important in sports?

title: Babe Ruth Says No to Baseball Comeback
story: Baseball legend Babe Ruth shocked fans in 1936. The Cincinnati Reds offered him a chance to play again, but Ruth said no! On March 9, the famous slugger decided his playing days were over. He had retired the year before and felt it was time to stay off the field. Ruth's choice marked the end of an incredible career that inspired millions.
activity_question: What team offered Babe Ruth a chance to play again?
follow_up_question: Why do you think Babe Ruth decided not to play baseball again?
